# Story 2.2: Extend MappingSuggestionService for Enum Hints

Status: ready-for-dev

<!-- Generated by BMAD Ultimate Story Context Engine -->
<!-- Story File: 2-2-extend-mappingsuggestionservice-for-enum-hints.md -->
<!-- Epic: 2 - AI-Powered Field Mapping Engine -->
<!-- Created: 2026-03-14 -->

## Story

As a SearchAI administrator,
I want LLM-powered mapping suggestions enhanced with enum coercion hints,
So that enum fields include value transformation guidance and business-friendly aliases.

## Acceptance Criteria

**Given** rule-based mapping didn't match all fields (coverage <100%)
**When** MappingSuggestionService generates LLM suggestions for unmapped fields
**Then** LLM prompt is extended to include enum coercion instructions: "For enum fields, suggest a valueMap transformation object mapping source values to canonical enum values"
**And** LLM prompt includes `enumPatterns` from `ConnectorTypeTemplate` for the connector type, with canonical enum values and display names
**And** LLM prompt includes discovered `enumValues` from `IDiscoveredSchemaField` (or `IConnectorSchemaField`) so the LLM can map source enum values to canonical enum values
**And** LLM response includes `suggestedAlias` and `suggestedLabel` for each mapping (already partially in prompt, parsing needs hardening)
**And** LLM response includes `transform` object with `type: 'value_map'` and `valueMap: {sourceValue: canonicalValue}` for enum fields
**And** service validates confidence scores are in 0.0-1.0 range, filters out suggestions with confidence < 0.5
**And** service uses existing circuit breaker pattern (already implemented) for LLM failures per FR64
**And** service falls back to empty suggestions array on LLM failure (graceful degradation per FR66)
**And** service sanitizes field names before LLM prompt (strip special chars, limit length) per NFR-S15 (already implemented)
**And** service uses `WorkerLLMClient` from `@agent-platform/llm` via `resolveIndexLLMConfig(tenantId, indexId)` — NOT direct Anthropic/OpenAI SDK imports (customer BYOK: bring-your-own-key per project/workspace)
**And** service supports any LLM provider configured by the tenant (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Azure, Cohere, etc.) via Vercel AI SDK abstraction
**And** service processing completes within 5 seconds per field (95th percentile) per NFR-P2

## Epic Context

**Epic 2 Goal:** Admins receive intelligent field mapping suggestions with confidence scores and can confirm/reject/edit mappings

**Epic 2 Stories (for cross-story context):**

- Story 2.1 (Wave 1a): Rule-Based Field Mapping Service -- pattern matching, 70% coverage target
- **Story 2.2** (THIS STORY, Wave 1b): Extend MappingSuggestionService for enum hints (LLM-powered, handles remaining 30%)
- Story 2.3 (BACKLOG): Confidence scoring + auto-apply logic (>=0.8 auto-confirmed)
- Story 2.4 (Wave 1c): FieldMapping CRUD with alias/enum storage (API endpoints)
- Story 2.5 (BACKLOG): Bulk confirm/reject mappings API
- Story 2.6 (BACKLOG): Manual field mapping creation
- Story 2.7 (BACKLOG): Mapping source tracking for analytics
- Story 2.8 (BACKLOG): Error handling with circuit breaker
- Story 2.9 (BACKLOG): FieldMappingSuggestionWorker orchestration (calls 2.1 then 2.2)

**This Story's Role:** This story enhances the **existing** LLM-powered `MappingSuggestionService` to produce richer suggestions. Story 2.9's worker will call RuleBasedMappingService (Story 2.1) FIRST for fast deterministic matching, then pass remaining unmapped fields to this service for LLM-powered suggestions with enum coercion hints, aliases, and value maps. The output `MappingSuggestion` interface is already defined and used by both services.

**Parallelization:** Wave 1b -- runs in parallel with Story 2.1 (Wave 1a) and Story 2.4 (Wave 1c). No cross-dependencies between wave 1 stories.

## File Structure & Locations

**Where to Modify Files:**

```
apps/search-ai/src/services/mapping-suggestion/
├── mapping-suggestion.service.ts          [MODIFY] - Migrate to WorkerLLMClient, extend buildPrompt(), parseResponse()
├── index.ts                               [NO CHANGE] - Exports already correct
├── __tests__/
│   ├── mapping-suggestion.service.test.ts [CREATE] - Unit tests

apps/search-ai/src/routes/
├── mappings.ts                            [MODIFY] - Pass tenantId to suggestMappings() call (line ~158)
```

**Call Chain (full picture):**

```
SDK: searchAiClient.suggestMappings(canonicalSchemaId, connectorId)
  → POST /api/mappings/suggest  (apps/search-ai/src/routes/mappings.ts L107)
    → tenantId = req.tenantContext!.tenantId  (L119, available but NOT currently passed)
    → indexId = req.body.indexId  (L117)
    → mappingSuggestionService.suggestMappings(indexId, request)  (L158, NEEDS tenantId added)
      → resolveIndexLLMConfig(indexId, 'mapping_suggestion')  (L177, WRONG SIGNATURE)
      → new Anthropic({apiKey})  (L243, MUST migrate to WorkerLLMClient)
```

**After this story, the call chain becomes:**

```
SDK: searchAiClient.suggestMappings(canonicalSchemaId, connectorId)
  → POST /api/mappings/suggest  (routes/mappings.ts)
    → tenantId = req.tenantContext!.tenantId
    → indexId = req.body.indexId
    → mappingSuggestionService.suggestMappings(tenantId, indexId, request)  // NEW: tenantId added
      → resolveIndexLLMConfig(tenantId, indexId)  // FIXED: correct signature
      → new WorkerLLMClient(provider, apiKey, modelId)  // MIGRATED: Vercel AI SDK
      → llmClient.chat(systemPrompt, messages)  // Provider-agnostic
```

**DO NOT modify:**

- `packages/search-ai-internal/src/canonical/connector-type-templates.ts` -- Templates are done (Story 1.6), read-only
- `packages/search-ai-sdk/src/client.ts` -- SDK passes tenantId via auth headers, no change needed
- `packages/database/src/models/` -- Models are done, no changes needed
- `packages/search-ai-internal/src/services/` -- Story 2.1's domain, not this story
- `apps/search-ai/src/workers/` -- Worker orchestration is Story 2.9

**Why `apps/search-ai/src/services/mapping-suggestion/`?** This service lives in the app layer because it depends on:

- `resolveIndexLLMConfig` (app-level LLM credential resolution)
- `WorkerLLMClient` from `@agent-platform/llm` (Vercel AI SDK wrapper — supports all providers)
- `getTemplateForConnector` (already imported from `@agent-platform/search-ai-internal/canonical`)

Services in `apps/search-ai/` MUST use `getLazyModel` for any Mongoose model access. However, this service does not access Mongoose models directly -- it receives data via `MappingSuggestionRequest`.

## Established Patterns (CRITICAL -- Follow These Exactly)

### Existing Service: MappingSuggestionService

```typescript
// apps/search-ai/src/services/mapping-suggestion/mapping-suggestion.service.ts
export class MappingSuggestionService {
  private circuitBreaker = new CircuitBreaker();

  async suggestMappings(
    indexId: string,
    request: MappingSuggestionRequest,
  ): Promise<MappingSuggestionResponse>;

  private async generateSuggestions(
    apiKey: string,
    request: MappingSuggestionRequest,
  ): Promise<MappingSuggestion[]>;

  private buildPrompt(request: MappingSuggestionRequest): string;
  private parseResponse(response: string): MappingSuggestion[];
  private isValidSuggestion(item: any): boolean;
  private sanitizeFields(fields: IConnectorSchemaField[] | ICanonicalField[]): Array<{...}>;
  private sanitizeString(str: string, maxLength: number): string;
}
```

This story modifies `buildPrompt()` and `parseResponse()` -- it does NOT change the class structure, circuit breaker, or `suggestMappings()` orchestration.

### MappingSuggestion Interface (Already Defined)

```typescript
export interface MappingSuggestion {
  canonicalField: string;
  sourcePath: string;
  transform: {
    type: 'direct' | 'lowercase' | 'uppercase' | 'split' | 'join' | 'parse_date' | 'value_map';
    valueMap?: Record<string, string>;
    delimiter?: string;
    sourceFormat?: string;
  };
  confidence: number;
  reasoning: string;
  suggestedAlias?: string;
  suggestedLabel?: string;
}
```

The interface already has `suggestedAlias`, `suggestedLabel`, `transform.valueMap`, and `transform.type: 'value_map'`. This story ensures the LLM prompt explicitly requests these and the parser correctly extracts them.

### MappingSuggestionRequest Interface (Already Defined)

```typescript
export interface MappingSuggestionRequest {
  sourceFields: IConnectorSchemaField[];
  canonicalFields: ICanonicalField[];
  connectorType: string;
  existingMappings?: IFieldMapping[];
}
```

### Input: IConnectorSchemaField

```typescript
// packages/database/src/models/connector-schema.model.ts
export interface IConnectorSchemaField {
  path: string;
  label: string;
  type: string;
  isCustom: boolean;
  isRequired: boolean;
  enumValues?: string[];
  sampleValues?: unknown[];
  children?: IConnectorSchemaField[];
  metadata?: any;
}
```

Note: `IConnectorSchemaField` has `enumValues?: string[]` -- this is the source enum data that the prompt must include.

### ConnectorTypeTemplate enumPatterns

```typescript
// packages/search-ai-internal/src/canonical/connector-type-templates.ts
export interface EnumPattern {
  values: string[];
  displayNames?: Record<string, string>;
}

export interface ConnectorTypeTemplate {
  // ...
  enumPatterns?: Record<string, EnumPattern>; // canonicalField → enum definition
}
```

Example: `ISSUE_TICKET.enumPatterns.priority` = `{values: ['critical','high','medium','low','trivial'], displayNames: {...}}`. The prompt must include these so the LLM knows the target canonical enum values when suggesting `valueMap` transforms.

### FieldMapping Model: Actual Field Names (READ BEFORE IMPLEMENTING)

**CRITICAL: The FieldMapping model uses different field names than what the epics AC reference.**

Actual model (`packages/database/src/models/field-mapping.model.ts`):

```typescript
interface IFieldMapping {
  sourcePath: string; // NOT "sourceField"
  canonicalField: string;
  transform: IFieldTransform;
  confidence: number;
  suggestedBy: string; // 'user' | 'llm' | 'rules' -- NOT "mappingSource"
  status: string; // 'suggested' | 'active' | 'rejected' -- NOT "pending"/"confirmed"
}
```

**Map epics AC terms to actual model fields:**

- Epics say `mappingSource: 'llm-generated'` --> Model uses `suggestedBy: 'llm'`
- Epics say `status: 'confirmed'` --> Model uses `status: 'active'`
- Epics say `status: 'pending'` --> Model uses `status: 'suggested'`
- Epics say `sourceField` --> Model uses `sourcePath`

**This story's service returns `MappingSuggestion` objects, NOT `IFieldMapping` documents.** The worker (Story 2.9) will map suggestions to `IFieldMapping` documents.

### Logger Pattern

```typescript
import { createLogger } from '@abl/compiler/platform';
const logger = createLogger('mapping-suggestion');
// Usage: logger.error('message', { context }) -- NOT pino-style
```

Already instantiated at module level in the existing service.

### Import Patterns

```typescript
// Current imports (will be modified):
import {
  type IConnectorSchemaField,
  type ICanonicalField,
  type IFieldMapping,
} from '@agent-platform/database/models';
import { getTemplateForConnector } from '@agent-platform/search-ai-internal/canonical';
import { resolveIndexLLMConfig } from '../llm-config/resolver.js';
import { createLogger } from '@abl/compiler/platform';
import Anthropic from '@anthropic-ai/sdk'; // REMOVE — replace with WorkerLLMClient

// NEW imports to add:
import { WorkerLLMClient } from '@agent-platform/llm';
```

**Key import change:** Remove `@anthropic-ai/sdk` direct import. Replace with `WorkerLLMClient` from `@agent-platform/llm` which wraps Vercel AI SDK and supports all 15+ providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Azure, Cohere, Groq, Mistral, etc.).

`getTemplateForConnector` already returns templates with `enumPatterns` — no new canonical imports needed.

- [ ] **Task 3: Extend buildPrompt() with enum coercion hints** (AC: enum prompt instructions)
  - [ ] Add enum pattern section to the prompt after the existing `templateHints` block
    - For each `enumPatterns` entry in the connector template, render: canonical field name, canonical values, display names
    - Format: structured list the LLM can reference when building `valueMap` objects
  - [ ] Extend source fields serialization to include `enumValues` when present on `IConnectorSchemaField`
    - Currently `sanitizeFields()` only includes `{path, label, type}` -- add `enumValues` (sanitized, limited to first 20 values, each truncated to 100 chars)
  - [ ] Add explicit enum coercion instruction to the prompt body:
    - "When a source field has enumValues and the target canonical field has enumPatterns, generate a valueMap transform that maps each source enum value to the closest canonical enum value"
    - "If no canonical enum exists for this field, use transform.type = 'direct'"
  - [ ] Strengthen the alias/label instruction (already partially present):
    - "suggestedAlias MUST be snake_case, max 50 chars, unique across all suggestions"
    - "suggestedLabel MUST be Title Case, max 100 chars"
  - [ ] Run `pnpm build --filter=@agent-platform/search-ai`

- [ ] **Task 4: Extend sanitizeFields() to include enum data** (AC: enum values in prompt)

  - [ ] Ensure no prompt injection through enum values (existing `sanitizeString` handles this)
  - [ ] Run `pnpm build --filter=@agent-platform/search-ai`

- [ ] **Task 5: Harden parseResponse() for valueMap and alias extraction** (AC: response parsing)
  - [ ] In `parseResponse()` `.map()`, add explicit extraction for `transform.valueMap`:
    - If `item.transform.type === 'value_map'` and `item.transform.valueMap` is an object, include it
    - Validate valueMap entries: both key and value must be strings, limit to 50 entries
    - If valueMap is invalid or missing when type is 'value_map', downgrade to `type: 'direct'`
  - [ ] Harden `suggestedAlias` extraction:
    - Validate: must be string, non-empty, max 50 chars, only `[a-z0-9_]` chars
    - If invalid, set to `undefined` (let downstream generate)
  - [ ] Harden `suggestedLabel` extraction:
    - Validate: must be string, non-empty, max 100 chars
    - If invalid, set to `undefined`
  - [ ] Extract `transform.sourceFormat` and `transform.delimiter` (already partially handled but verify)
  - [ ] Run `pnpm build --filter=@agent-platform/search-ai`

- [ ] **Task 6: Add isValidValueMap() helper** (AC: transform validation)
  - [ ] Create private method `isValidValueMap(valueMap: unknown): valueMap is Record<string, string>`
    - Check: is non-null object, not array
    - Check: all keys are strings, all values are strings
    - Check: max 50 entries (prevent oversized maps)
  - [ ] Create private method `sanitizeAlias(alias: unknown): string | undefined`
    - Validate and normalize: lowercase, replace non-alphanumeric with underscore, trim leading/trailing underscores, max 50 chars
    - Return undefined if result is empty
  - [ ] Run `pnpm build --filter=@agent-platform/search-ai`

## Dev Notes

### What This Story Actually Changes

This story enhances the existing service AND fixes the LLM client architecture:

1. **`generateSuggestions()`** -- Replace direct `Anthropic` SDK with `WorkerLLMClient` from `@agent-platform/llm`. This enables BYOK (bring-your-own-key) support — tenants use their own LLM provider (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Azure, etc.).
2. **`suggestMappings()`** -- Add `tenantId` parameter. Fix `resolveIndexLLMConfig` call to use correct `(tenantId, indexId)` signature.
3. **`buildPrompt()`** -- Add 2 new sections: (a) enum pattern hints from `ConnectorTypeTemplate.enumPatterns`, (b) source field enum values in the serialized field data. Add explicit enum coercion and alias/label formatting instructions.
4. **`sanitizeFields()`** -- Extend return type to include `enumValues` for source fields that have them.
5. **`parseResponse()`** -- Harden extraction of `transform.valueMap`, `suggestedAlias`, `suggestedLabel`. Add validation helpers.
6. **New private helpers** -- `isValidValueMap()`, `sanitizeAlias()`.

The class structure, circuit breaker, and singleton export pattern are unchanged. The `suggestMappings()` signature gains a `tenantId` parameter.

### Route Call Site: mappings.ts (MUST UPDATE)

```typescript
// apps/search-ai/src/routes/mappings.ts — POST /suggest handler (line ~158)
// CURRENT (broken):
const suggestionResult = await mappingSuggestionService.suggestMappings(indexId, {
  sourceFields: connectorSchema.fields,
  canonicalFields: canonicalSchema.fields,
  connectorType: connectorSchema.connectorId,
  existingMappings: existingMappings as any[],
});

// AFTER (fixed — pass tenantId):
const suggestionResult = await mappingSuggestionService.suggestMappings(tenantId, indexId, {
  sourceFields: connectorSchema.fields,
  canonicalFields: canonicalSchema.fields,
  connectorType: connectorSchema.connectorId,
  existingMappings: existingMappings as any[],
});
```

**WARNING: Story 2.4 also modifies `mappings.ts`.** Since Wave 1b and 1c run in parallel, coordinate the merge. Story 2.2 changes ONE line (~158) in the `POST /suggest` handler. Story 2.4 ADDS new endpoints. These should not conflict.

### WorkerLLMClient Pattern (from vocabulary-generation-worker.ts)

```typescript
// Established pattern for creating LLM clients in search-ai:
async function createLLMClientForJob(tenantId: string, indexId: string): Promise<WorkerLLMClient> {
  const llmConfig = await resolveIndexLLMConfig(tenantId, indexId);
  return new WorkerLLMClient(
    llmConfig.provider,
    llmConfig.apiKey,
    llmConfig.useCases.vocabularyGeneration?.model || 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514',
  );
}
```

Follow this pattern but for the `mapping_suggestion` use case.

### LLM Client Migration: Anthropic SDK → WorkerLLMClient (REQUIRED)

**The existing `MappingSuggestionService` uses `Anthropic` from `@anthropic-ai/sdk` directly (line 17) and instantiates it in `generateSuggestions()` (line 243) with a hardcoded model ID.** This is wrong — the platform uses BYOK (bring-your-own-key) where customers configure their own LLM providers per project/workspace. A tenant may use OpenAI, Google, Azure, Cohere, or any of the 15+ supported providers.

**This story MUST migrate `generateSuggestions()` to use `WorkerLLMClient`:**

1. Remove `import Anthropic from '@anthropic-ai/sdk'`
2. Add `import { WorkerLLMClient } from '@agent-platform/llm'`
3. Use `resolveIndexLLMConfig(tenantId, indexId)` to resolve the tenant's configured LLM credentials
4. Create `WorkerLLMClient(provider, apiKey, modelId)` from the resolved config
5. Call `llmClient.chat(systemPrompt, messages)` instead of `client.messages.create()`
6. Handle the case where no LLM is configured (return empty suggestions gracefully)

**Reference implementation pattern** (from `question-synthesis-worker.ts` and other workers):

```typescript
// 1. Resolve tenant's configured LLM
const llmConfig = await resolveIndexLLMConfig(tenantId, indexId);
const useCaseConfig = llmConfig.useCases?.mapping_suggestion;

if (!useCaseConfig?.apiKey) {
  logger.warn('No LLM configured for mapping suggestions', { tenantId, indexId });
  return { suggestions: [], coverage: 0 };
}

// 2. Create provider-agnostic client
const llmClient = new WorkerLLMClient(
  useCaseConfig.provider, // 'anthropic' | 'openai' | 'google' | etc.
  useCaseConfig.apiKey, // Decrypted from LLMCredential
  useCaseConfig.model?.id, // Resolved model ID for this tier
);

// 3. Call via Vercel AI SDK abstraction
const result = await llmClient.chat(systemPrompt, [{ role: 'user', content: prompt }]);
```

### resolveIndexLLMConfig Signature Fix (REQUIRED)

The existing service calls `resolveIndexLLMConfig(indexId, 'mapping_suggestion')` but the actual function signature is `resolveIndexLLMConfig(tenantId: string, indexId: string)`. **This MUST be fixed** as part of the migration.

The `suggestMappings()` method signature needs `tenantId` added as a parameter (or the `MappingSuggestionRequest` interface extended to include it). The worker (Story 2.9) will provide `tenantId` from the job data.

### LLM Infrastructure Overview (for context)

The platform's LLM resolution uses a 4-tier hierarchy:

1. **Per-Index LLM Config** (`SearchIndex.llmConfig.useCases.mapping_suggestion`)
2. **Use Case Defaults** (maps use cases to model tiers: fast/balanced/powerful)
3. **Tenant LLM Policy + LLM Credentials** (per-tenant encrypted API keys, allowed providers)
4. **Environment Variables** (dev/testing fallback only)

Key files:

- `packages/llm/src/worker-llm-client.ts` — `WorkerLLMClient` class (wraps Vercel AI SDK `generateText`)
- `packages/llm/src/provider-factory.ts` — `createVercelProvider()` supporting 15+ providers
- `apps/search-ai/src/services/llm-config/resolver.ts` — `resolveIndexLLMConfig(tenantId, indexId)`
- `apps/search-ai/src/services/llm-config/defaults.ts` — Use case → model tier defaults
- `packages/database/src/models/llm-credential.model.ts` — Encrypted per-tenant API keys
- `packages/database/src/models/tenant-model.model.ts` — Multi-tier model config with connections

### Testing Strategy

Since `buildPrompt()` and `parseResponse()` are private methods, tests should either:

1. **Test via `suggestMappings()`** with a mocked `WorkerLLMClient` (preferred for integration coverage)
2. **Use TypeScript type assertion** to access private methods for focused unit tests: `(service as any).buildPrompt(request)`

The existing service has no tests -- this story creates the first test file. Use `vi.mock('@agent-platform/llm')` to mock `WorkerLLMClient` and `vi.mock('../llm-config/resolver.js')` to mock config resolution.

### What This Story Does NOT Include

- Creating a new service (this modifies the existing `MappingSuggestionService`)
- Persisting FieldMapping documents to MongoDB (Story 2.3/2.9)
- Worker orchestration calling rule-based then LLM (Story 2.9)
- API endpoints (Story 2.4-2.6)
- Adding a new circuit breaker (already exists)

## Previous Story Intelligence (Epic 1)

**From Epic 1 retrospective (2026-03-14):**

1. **`getLazyModel` is mandatory in `apps/search-ai/`** -- This service does not access Mongoose models directly. It receives data via `MappingSuggestionRequest` parameters. No `getLazyModel` needed in this story, but do NOT add any direct model imports.
2. **ESM imports with `.js` extension required** -- All relative imports must use `.js` extension. The existing service already follows this pattern.
3. **`err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)` error pattern** -- Already used in the existing service (line 218). Maintain this pattern in any new error handling.
4. **Build with `pnpm build --filter=@agent-platform/search-ai`** -- Package name includes the app prefix. Build after every significant change.
5. **Test with vitest** -- Use `vi.mock()` for module mocking. Since this service uses direct imports (not dependency injection), mocking requires `vi.mock()` at the module level.
6. **Connector-type templates already work** -- `getTemplateForConnector()` is already imported and used in `buildPrompt()`. This story extends its usage to include `enumPatterns`.

**From Story 2.1 (parallel development):**

7. **Output format compatibility** -- Both RuleBasedMappingService (Story 2.1) and this service produce objects compatible with the `MappingSuggestion` interface. Story 2.9's worker merges both result sets. Ensure no breaking changes to the `MappingSuggestion` interface.

## Project Context References

**Essential Reading Before Implementation:**

1. **`apps/search-ai/src/services/mapping-suggestion/mapping-suggestion.service.ts`** -- MUST READ. The file being modified. Understand every method before changing anything.
2. **`packages/llm/src/worker-llm-client.ts`** -- MUST READ. `WorkerLLMClient` class — constructor signature, `chat()` method, timeout handling.
3. **`packages/llm/src/provider-factory.ts`** -- MUST READ. `createVercelProvider()` — how providers are resolved, 15+ supported providers.
4. **`apps/search-ai/src/services/llm-config/resolver.ts`** -- MUST READ. `resolveIndexLLMConfig(tenantId, indexId)` — return type with `useCases`, `apiKey`, `provider`, `model`.
5. **`apps/search-ai/src/services/llm-config/defaults.ts`** -- READ. Use case defaults (add `mapping_suggestion` if missing).
6. **`apps/search-ai/src/workers/question-synthesis-worker.ts`** -- READ. Reference pattern for WorkerLLMClient usage in search-ai workers.
7. **`packages/search-ai-internal/src/canonical/connector-type-templates.ts`** -- MUST READ. `EnumPattern` interface and all 8 templates' `enumPatterns` dictionaries.
8. **`packages/database/src/models/connector-schema.model.ts`** -- READ. `IConnectorSchemaField` interface with `enumValues?: string[]`.
9. **`packages/database/src/models/field-mapping.model.ts`** -- READ. `IFieldTransform` interface (target transform structure).
10. **`packages/database/src/models/llm-credential.model.ts`** -- READ. How tenant LLM credentials are stored/decrypted.
11. **`docs/searchai/stories/implementation-artifacts/epic-1-retro-2026-03-14.md`** -- READ. Lessons learned.
12. **`docs/searchai/stories/implementation-artifacts/2-1-rule-based-field-mapping-service.md`** -- READ. Parallel story for output format compatibility.
